title: Signals and Systems - Problems
summary: This page contains problem figures and problem statements from Chapter 1 on Signals and Systems. It includes mechanical, electrical, and fluid system diagrams (Figures P1.8-3 through P1.8-6) along with problem instructions related to state equations and circuit analysis. Problems involve expressing circuit variables in terms of state variables and writing state equations for higher-order circuits.

figures.caption: A separately excited DC motor driven by a field circuit with voltage input x(t), field resistance R_f, and field inductance L_f, coupled to a rotor with moment of inertia J and damping coefficient B, with constant armature current i_a producing angular displacement θ.
figures.description: The figure is a schematic of a separately excited DC motor with no plotted axes, showing an electrical field circuit on the left and a mechanical rotor on the right. The input voltage x(t) drives field current i_f through field resistance R_f and field inductance L_f; the motor is also labeled with constant armature current i_a. The rotor is modeled with angular displacement θ, moment of inertia J, and viscous damping B, indicating how the electrical excitation is coupled to rotational motion. Students should notice the left-to-right energy conversion from input voltage and field circuit dynamics to mechanical shaft motion.
